title: podman: remember hooks-dir on restarts
body:
When podman restarts config values within the Engine are lost. Add --hook-dirs arguments as appropriate to the cleanup command so that hooks are preserved on restarts due to the on-restart setting

Fixes: #17935

I've confirmed this works with my reproducer, and it's probably the smallest change that'd work for us so opening PR with that for feedback.
Thanks!

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
Fix hooks not being run properly after first restart if a non-default --hooks-dir setting was used
```
(happy to remove this note as I don't think it's a critical bug, but it's technically user-facing so wrote it in doubt)

comments.body:
Thanks, I was just looking at the CI error telling me to add a test and wondering where would be good.
030-run.bats looks good to me, thanks for the hint about wait_for_restart_count as well, I was about to forget podman detaches on first restart..

I've run out of time for today to properly share that code out but will update the PR tomorrow with a test.
LGTM once a test is added

Thanks!

I've just added a test and confirmed locally that it fails with current podman (2 lines instead of 6 in file) / works with this PR; letting CI runs and will look if something fails.

EDIT: added skip_if_remote call as --hooks-dir is not accepted with podman remote

The remaining test failure seems unrelated:
> [+0887s]   [FAIL] Podman kube play [It] secret as volume support - multiple volumes

I'll ignore it unless you want me to do a trivial rebase to retrigger builds or something

Thanks,
